---
fettitle: "EM_6bp_analysis"
author: "Trish"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:



```{r load libraries}

library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(openxlsx)
library(BiocManager)
library(TFBSTools)
library(JASPAR2024)
library(tibble)
library(RSQLite)
```

```{r load data}

EM6_files <- list.files("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/FinaleDB/endmotifs/EM_6bp", pattern = "EM6$", full.names = TRUE)
  
EM6 <- EM6_files %>%
  set_names(basename(EM6_files)) %>%
  map_dfr(
    ~ read.delim(.x, header = FALSE), 
    .id = "file"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    end_motif = V1, 
    frequency = V2
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = if_else(
      str_detect(file, "^Ascites"),
      "cancer",
      "control"
    )
    )
  
end_motif_means <- EM6%>%
  group_by(end_motif, treatment)%>%
  summarise(mean_freq = mean(frequency))%>%
  ungroup()


differences_df <- end_motif_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean_freq)%>%
  mutate(diff = (cancer - control)) %>%
  arrange(desc(diff))

top_differences <- bind_rows(
  differences_df %>% slice_head(n = 10),
  differences_df %>% slice_tail(n = 10)
) %>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = end_motif))

#getting 10 biggest cancer drops as a list
top_20_cancer_drops <- top_differences %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(end_motif)%>%
  as.character()

writeLines(top_20_cancer_drops, "top_20_cancer_drops.txt")


# making a lollipop graph to show biggest differences

lollipop6EM <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = top_differences, aes(x = end_motif, y = cancer, colour = "Mean Cancer Values"), size = 3) +
 geom_point(data = top_differences, aes(x = end_motif, y = control, colour = "Mean Control Values"), size = 3) +
  geom_segment(data = top_differences, aes(x = end_motif, xend = end_motif, y = cancer, yend = control), colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  scale_colour_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c("Mean Cancer Values"= "magenta",
               "Mean Control Values" = "blue")) +
  labs(y = "Relative Frequencies",
       x = "End Motif",
       title = "End Motifs showing the biggest differences between \ncontrol and cancer conditions",
       subtitle = "Top 10 differences where cancer is greater & Top 10 differences where cancer is lower") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 
  

ggsave("lollipop6EM.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)

```
```{r}

#to get a list of end-motifs with top differences

top_list <- top_differences %>%
  pull(end_motif)


selected_samples <- EM6[EM6$end_motif %in% top_list,]
selected_samples


ggplot(data = selected_samples, aes(x = end_motif, 
                                    y = frequency,
                                    fill = treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4) + #position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "End Motif",
       y = "Frequency",
       title = "Mean Values for Cancer and Control Samples",
       subtitle = "Top Ten differences where cancer higher and\n Top Ten where cancer lower ", 
       fill = "Treatment") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Treatment",
    values = c("cancer"= "magenta",
               "control" = "blue")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey90"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        legend.key.width = unit(0.2, "cm"))

ggsave("boxplot6EM.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
    
```
```{r making a heatmap}

heatmapEM6_df <- EM6 %>%
  select(file, end_motif, frequency) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = file,
              values_from = frequency)

heatmapEM6.mat <- heatmapEM6_df %>%
  column_to_rownames("end_motif") %>%
  as.matrix()


pheatmap(heatmapEM6.mat, 
         fontsize_row = 2, 
         cutree_rows = 4, 
         filename = "overall_6EM_heatmap.png")

         
```
```{r finding rownames in top cluster}

hc <- hclust(dist(heatmapEM6.mat))

clusters <- cutree(hc, k = 4)

cluster1_rows <- rownames(heatmapEM6.mat)[clusters==1]
cluster1_rows

cluster1_mat <- heatmapEM6.mat[clusters == 4, ]

pheatmap(cluster1_mat,
         filename = "heatmap_detailing_oppositeTop_EM6.png")



```
```{r exploring heatmaps with zscores}

pheatmap(heatmapEM6.mat,
         scale = "row",
         show_rownames = FALSE,
         filename = "6EM_zscoresbyrow.png")

pheatmap(heatmapEM6.mat,
         scale = "column",
         show_rownames = FALSE,
         filename = "6EM_zscoresbycolumn.png")

```

```{r analysis to find which of the differences are significant}

#finding absolute differences and calculating log2FX
signif_analysis_df <- end_motif_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = treatment, values_from = mean_freq)%>%
  mutate(abs_diff = abs(cancer - control),
         log2FX = log2(cancer/control)) %>%
  arrange(desc(control))


#calculating pvalues
motif_tests <- EM6 %>%
  group_by(end_motif)%>%
  summarise(
    p.value = wilcox.test(frequency~treatment)$p.value )%>%
    ungroup()

#correction for multiple testing
motif_tests <- motif_tests %>%
  mutate(FDR = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"))

#making a big table
final_motif_results <- signif_analysis_df %>%
  left_join(motif_tests, by="end_motif") %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_diff)) %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  #slice(1:50) %>%
  mutate(end_motif = factor(end_motif, levels = unique(end_motif)), direction = if_else(log2FX > 0, "cancer-enriched", "control-enriched"))
  

#making a vector of these cancer enriched signigicant 6bp end-motifs
cancerSigDiff_6bp_EM <- final_motif_results %>%
  filter(direction == "cancer-enriched") #%>%
  #pull(end_motif)%>%
  #as.character()

#making a vector of those control enriched signif 6bp end-motifs
controlSigDiff_6bp_EM <- final_motif_results %>%
  filter(direction == "control-enriched") %>%
  pull(end_motif) %>%
  as.character()


```

```{r creating meme files for tomtom}


#write a function to create a matrix for 6bp end-motifs
write_meme_file <- function(motifs, filename = "top50sigDiff_6bp_EM.meme"){
  
bases <- c("A", "C", "G", "T")

con <- file(filename, open = "wt")
on.exit(close(con))

writeLines(
  c(
    "MEME version 4",
    "",
    "ALPHABET = ACGT",
    "",
    "strands: + -",
    "",
    "Background letter frequencies", 
    "A 0.25 C 0.25 G 0.25 T 0.25",
    ""
  ),
  con
)

for (motif in motifs) {
  motif_bases <- strsplit(motif, " ")[[1]]
  
  motif_matrix <- t(
    sapply(motif_bases, function(base) {
      as.numeric(bases == base)
    })
  )
  
  writeLines(paste("MOTIF", motif), con)
  
  writeLines(
    "letter-probability matrix: alength= 4 w= 6 nsites= 1 E= 0",
    con
  )
  write.table(
    motif_matrix, 
    file = con,
    quote = FALSE,
    row.names = FALSE,
    col.names = FALSE
  )
  writeLines("", con)
}
  
}

#run the function for cancer-enriched
write_meme_file(
  motifs = cancerSigDiff_6bp_EM,
  filename = "cancersigDiff_6bp_EM.meme"
)

#run the function for control-enriched
write_meme_file(
  motifs = controlSigDiff_6bp_EM,
  filename = "controlsigDiff_6bp_EM.meme"
)

```

```{r analysing output from tomtom}

#making a dataframe with results from cancer enrichment analysis

Cancer6bpEM <- read.delim("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/R_Code/Cancer6bpEM.tsv") 

Cancer6bpEM <- Cancer6bpEM %>%
  filter(q.value < 0.05)

#setting up to utilise JASPAR2024 package

con <- DBI::dbConnect(
  RSQLite::SQLite(),
  JASPAR2024::db(JASPAR2024())
)
#extracting target_ids from tomtom readout
target_ids <- unique(Cancer6bpEM$Target_ID)

jaspar_matches <- lapply(target_ids, function(matrix_id){
  result <- TFBSTools::getMatrixSet(
    con,
    list(
      ID = matrix_id,
      all_versions = TRUE
    )
  )
  if (length(result)==0) {return(NULL)}
  result[[1]]
})

names(jaspar_matches) <- target_ids
jaspar_matches <- jaspar_matches[!vapply(jaspar_matches, is.null, logical(1))]

DBI::dbDisconnect(con)



Cancer6bpEM_tf <- cancerSigDiff_6bp_EM %>%
  inner_join(Cancer6bpEM, by = c("end_motif" = "Query_ID"))%>%
  inner_join(jaspar_annotation, by = "Target_ID")

jaspar_annotations <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(jaspar_matches, function(motif) {
    motif_tags <- tags(motif)

    data.frame(
      Target_ID = ID(motif),
      TF_name = name(motif),
      species = paste(motif_tags$species, collapse = "; "),
      tax_group = paste(motif_tags$tax_group, collapse = "; "),
      family = paste(motif_tags$family, collapse = "; "),
      class = paste(motif_tags$class, collapse = "; "),
      collection = paste(motif_tags$collection, collapse = "; "),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
)

jaspar_annotations

TFs_of_interest <- jaspar_annotations %>%
  filter(species=="Homo sapiens")

TFfamilies <- unique(TFs_of_interest$family)

FOX_TF <- TFs_of_interest %>%
  filter(family=="FOX")

```
```{r uploading results from tomtom}

#uploading cancer tsv 
Cancer6bpEM <- read.delim("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/R_Code/Cancer6bpEM.tsv") 

#saving it as csv file 
write.csv(Cancer6bpEM_tf,
          "Cancer6pEM_tf.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

#uploading control tsv file
Control6bpEM <- read.delim("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/R_Code/Control6bpEM.tsv")

#saving it as a csv file
write.csv(Control6bpEM,
          "Control6bpEM_tf.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r working backwards to get 6bp EM associated with FOX tf}






Cancer6bpEM_FOX<- 
  inner_join(Cancer6bpEM, FOX_TF, by ="Target_ID")



```
